regions.eastern-caribbean: Eastern Caribbean briefing for March 29, 2026: Current conditions in San Juan show 75°F with light ENE winds at 5 knots under overcast skies and minimal rain chance (13%). Expect increasing NE winds up to 11-13 knots with overcast skies and rising rain probability through Day 1 (68%) and continuing showers Days 2 and 3, with rain chances near 75%. Mariners should prepare for periods of reduced visibility and localized wet decks, especially near St. Thomas, St. Croix, and Turks & Caicos where similar conditions prevail. Yacht and fishing captains should secure loose gear and monitor forecasts for potential squalls; cruise passengers are advised to plan indoor activities during heavier showers. No small craft or gale warnings currently in effect.
regions.western-caribbean: Western Caribbean update for March 29, 2026: Cozumel and surrounding ports are enjoying mild NE winds at 10-14 knots with temperatures near 77-79°F and partly cloudy skies today. Scattered rain showers will increase tonight into Monday, with a 30-55% chance of rain and winds shifting ENE to E at 12-14 knots. Expect overcast skies and diminishing rain chances by Tuesday, with seas remaining moderate. Mariners should secure light gear and monitor local updates; fishing captains should plan for reduced visibility and occasional showers near Costa Maya and Belize City on Monday. Anchorage conditions remain stable, but small craft operators should remain vigilant for increasing winds and brief rain squalls, especially near Roatan and Grand Cayman.
regions.bahamas: Bahamas region weather for March 29, 2026: Currently in Nassau, clear skies with light northeast winds at 6 knots and 69°F. Expect increasing northeast winds up to 13 knots today with overcast skies and minimal rain chances (4%), favoring safe cruising and fishing conditions across Nassau, Freeport, Bimini, Berry Islands, and Turks & Caicos. By tomorrow and Thursday, east winds strengthen to 23-24 knots with drizzle and a higher chance of light rain (up to 32%), warranting caution for smaller vessels and those anchoring in exposed areas like Bimini and the Berry Islands. Mariners should secure loose gear and monitor conditions for potential small craft advisories as winds increase later in the week.
regions.southern-caribbean: Southern Caribbean conditions today remain stable with temperatures near 79-80°F and steady ENE winds around 13-14 knots. Expect partly cloudy skies at Aruba and Curaçao with minimal rain chances (under 10%), while Cartagena and Bonaire should see similar conditions. Over the next 72 hours, increasing cloud cover and scattered showers will develop, with the highest rain probability (up to 53%) by March 31, especially near Cartagena. Mariners should note moderate easterly trades supporting good sailing conditions but monitor localized showers if operating near exposed anchorages in Cartagena; fishing fleets should plan for intermittent rain and variable visibility. Passengers are advised to keep light rain gear handy but can expect mostly comfortable cruising weather through the weekend.
regions.central-caribbean: Central Caribbean conditions remain breezy with east-northeast winds 16-19 knots and scattered showers, especially near Grand Cayman, where rain chances peak near 80% today. Expect intermittent rain showers with partly cloudy skies through March 31, easing slightly by day three. Mariners in Roatan, Belize City, Cozumel, and Costa Maya should prepare for choppy seas and maintain vigilance for localized squalls; no small craft advisories currently, but conditions could challenge smaller vessels. Cruise and fishing captains are advised to secure deck gear and monitor updated forecasts for timing of showers and wind shifts when planning nearshore operations or anchoring in exposed areas.
regions.lesser-antilles: Lesser Antilles weather for March 29, 2026: Barbados reports 79°F with ENE winds at 8 knots under partly cloudy skies and no rain currently. Expect a slight uptick in showers today with 15% rain chance and winds increasing to 11 knots ENE by afternoon. Similar conditions with overcast skies and light rain chances near 13-18% persist through March 31, with winds steady from the ENE around 11-14 knots. Mariners should anticipate generally smooth seas; small craft operators near St. Lucia and Martinique should monitor for localized showers reducing visibility. Passengers advised to carry light rain gear for intermittent showers, while fishing captains can expect stable ENE trades favorable for morning operations but should remain alert for brief rain bands. Anchorage conditions remain good across primary ports with no marine warnings issued.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles and adjacent ports will see mild conditions today with overcast skies, light NNE winds at 2 knots, and no rain expected. Temperatures rise to 79 degrees with westerly winds increasing to 10 knots tomorrow, maintaining overcast skies and dry conditions. By day three, expect a slight chance of light rain near Catalina Island with southwest winds at 11 knots, so keep an eye on updated forecasts. Yacht and fishing captains should plan for generally calm seas and moderate winds; passengers should dress in layers due to variable cloud cover and mild temperatures.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada region conditions are stable with light northerly winds at 2 knots and overcast skies this morning, temperature at 61°F. Expect afternoon fog with southwest winds increasing to 8-9 knots through Punta Banda and Islas Todos Santos, reducing visibility especially early in the day. Rain chances remain minimal below 7 percent over the next three days, so no significant marine weather threats are forecast. Captains should maintain caution navigating near shorelines during fog, using radar and sound signals as visibility may drop below safe limits, especially in the Islas Todos Santos anchorage.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas region conditions remain stable today with steady northwest winds at 9-12 knots and temperatures near 74-76°F. Overcast skies will persist through Day 1 with no precipitation expected, shifting to partly cloudy conditions Days 2 and 3. Mariners should expect smooth seas and good visibility for navigation and fishing; anchorage at Cabo San Lucas and San Jose del Cabo remains safe with no small craft advisories. Passengers should dress in layers due to moderate temperatures and light winds, with no weather-related interruptions anticipated for port operations.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan and nearby ports including Topolobampo and Altata are experiencing mild conditions today with light SSW winds at 3 knots and partly cloudy skies, temperatures steady near 77 degrees Fahrenheit. Over the next three days expect gradually increasing west-northwest winds up to 11 knots with persistent overcast skies and minimal rain chances under 5 percent. Mariners should find smooth seas and no marine wind warnings in effect, making it safe for routine operations and anchorage in all local ports. Cruise and fishing captains can plan normal activities but monitor for slight wind shifts and cloud cover that may reduce afternoon visibility.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta region is currently overcast with light northeast winds near 3 knots and minimal rain chance today. Expect a shift to west-northwest winds increasing to 10 knots tomorrow with scattered rain showers and a 55% rain probability, particularly affecting open waters near Punta Mita and Chacala. By day three, winds will remain WNW at 7-8 knots with mostly overcast skies and low rain risk. Mariners should secure vessels and monitor local updates; fishing captains should plan for reduced visibility and occasional showers tomorrow, while cruise passengers can expect mild conditions with brief rain interruptions. Anchorage remains safe with no Small Craft Advisories or warnings in effect.
regions.western-mediterranean: Western Mediterranean briefing for March 29, 2026: Barcelona reports clear skies, 51°F, with light NNW winds at 4 knots and no rain expected today. Expect increasing northeasterly winds to 14 knots tomorrow with thunderstorms and an 83% rain chance—vessels should prepare for reduced visibility and possible sudden gusts, especially when approaching Barcelona, Valencia, and Palma de Mallorca. By day three, winds shift to the southwest at 8 knots with overcast skies and no rain, improving conditions for fishing and leisure cruising near Ibiza and Malaga. Captains should secure loose items and monitor updated forecasts closely; passengers are advised to carry rain gear and exercise caution in port areas during tomorrow’s storm threat.
regions.central-mediterranean: Central Mediterranean briefing for March 29, 2026: Naples currently reports light NNE winds at 3 knots, 53°F, partly cloudy, with no rain expected today. Expect overcast skies and NE winds near 9 knots by afternoon, remaining dry. By March 30 and 31, rain showers will develop with increasing winds from WNW then NE at 6 to 12 knots and rain probabilities exceeding 85 percent. Yacht and fishing captains should plan for wet decks and reduced visibility from showers starting tomorrow, especially near Naples and Civitavecchia. Cruise passengers in all ports including Marseille, Genoa, and Dubrovnik should carry rain gear and anticipate soggy conditions by midweek; anchorage remains stable with no small craft advisories at this time.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Eastern Mediterranean briefing for March 29, 2026: Athens (Piraeus) currently holds mild conditions at 57°F with light WSW winds at 3 knots and partly cloudy skies; no rain expected today. Over the next three days, expect increasing cloud cover with light to moderate westerly to southerly winds and patchy fog developing on days two and three, raising visibility concerns for navigation near Athens, Santorini, and Mykonos. Fishing and yacht captains should exercise caution during morning hours in fog-prone areas, particularly around Saronic and Aegean anchorages, and plan for reduced visibility near Istanbul and Izmir. Cruise passengers should anticipate mostly dry weather but carry light layers and be prepared for limited visibility during early mornings and late evenings in ports from Rhodes to Haifa.
